examples.title: If-Else to Check Positive Number
examples.description: Simple program demonstrating if-else statement to check if a number is positive
examples.explanation: This program uses if-else to check if entered number is positive. If num > 0 evaluates to true, the if block prints positive message; otherwise the else block executes. The condition uses relational operator > for comparison.

examples.title: Else-If Ladder for Grade Calculation
examples.description: Program to calculate grade based on marks using else-if ladder
examples.explanation: The else-if ladder checks conditions sequentially from highest to lowest. The first condition that evaluates to true executes its block, and remaining conditions are skipped. This is efficient as conditions are checked in order.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - worst case checks all conditions

examples.title: Conditional Operator for Maximum
examples.description: Using ternary operator to find maximum of two numbers
examples.explanation: The ternary operator (?:) provides a compact way to find maximum. The condition (a > b) is checked; if true, a is assigned to max, otherwise b is assigned. Equivalent to if-else but more concise for simple assignments.

examples.title: Nested If-Else for Greatest of Three
examples.description: Finding largest of three numbers using nested if-else
examples.explanation: This uses nested if-else to compare three numbers. First it compares a and b to find which is larger, then compares that result with c. The outer if checks a > b, inner blocks handle remaining comparisons.

examples.title: Leap Year Check Using Decision Making
examples.description: Program to determine if a year is a leap year using if-else ladder
examples.explanation: A leap year is divisible by 400 OR divisible by 4 but not by 100. The else-if ladder checks these conditions in order of priority: first check for 400 (highest priority), then 100, then 4. This correctly handles all edge cases.
